migrationSteps.title: Update target framework
migrationSteps.description: Change the target framework moniker in your .csproj file from net6.0 to net8.0.

migrationSteps.title: Update NuGet packages
migrationSteps.description: Update all Microsoft.AspNetCore and Microsoft.Extensions packages to their 8.0.x versions.
migrationSteps.codeChanges:
dotnet add package Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.JwtBearer --version 8.0.0
dotnet add package Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore --version 8.0.0

migrationSteps.title: Replace deprecated APIs
migrationSteps.description: Several APIs have been removed or replaced in .NET 8. Update your code to use the new alternatives.

breakingChanges: UseEndpoints() is no longer needed — use top-level route registration instead
System.Text.Json now requires explicit JsonSerializerOptions for some scenarios that previously used defaults
Minimal API parameter binding has changed — [FromBody] may be needed where it was implicit
estimatedEffort: Medium — 2-4 hours for a typical project with 10-20 controllers and EF Core data access.
nextStepSuggestion: Generate updated integration code for the new .NET 8 APIs.
nextStepDescription: Now that you have a migration plan, the Code Generator can create updated boilerplate code using .NET 8 patterns like minimal APIs and the new authentication middleware.
